title: Heptapleurum wallichianum
taxonomy: Heptapleurum wallichianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. The Araliaceae family is a group of plants commonly known as the ginseng or ivy family. This family comprises around 1,800 species of flowering plants, including some that are widely cultivated for their medicinal and ornamental value. The specific classification of Heptapleurum wallichianum is Heptapleurum wallichianum (Hook.) Seem., under the family Araliaceae.
uses: Heptapleurum wallichianum, also known as the Himalayan maidenhair fern, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in Asia. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses.
